The drive to be consistent is activated by reminding people of their existing commitments, that is, their prior statements, choices, or actions on the topic under consideration. But how can we uncover a person's existing commitments? One way would be to look the person up online where they are happy to provide evidence of who they are and what they have preferred, valued, said, and done in the past. Another way is to just ask them preliminary questions about the commitments that they've made that are relevant to the choices we would like them to make in the future. The learning of their hobbies, personal interests, or charitable activities reveals what they value. To harness the principle of consistency ethically, we should shape our proposal to be truly consistent with what their existing commitments tell us is most important to them.
